workouts.title: Full Body Power Session
workouts.coachNote: Every rep is a deposit into your future. Stay locked in, control your breathing, and attack each set.

exercises.name: Jump Rope
exercises.note: Stay on your toes — light feet build fast feet.

exercises.name: Hip Circle Rotations
exercises.note: Open the hips before any loaded movement.

exercises.name: Arm Circles
exercises.note: Loosen the shoulders — big circles down to small.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
exercises.note: Sit back, chest up, knees track over toes.

exercises.name: Inchworm to Push-Up
exercises.note: Slow and controlled — activate the full chain.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.note: Drive through the floor — power starts from the ground up.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Hinge at the hips, soft knee, feel the hamstring load.

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.note: Stay tall, big stride, drive the knee forward.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.note: Control the descent — explosive press, not a drop and bounce.

exercises.name: Bent-Over Barbell Row
exercises.note: Pull with your elbows, not your hands.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.note: Neutral grip option reduces shoulder stress.

exercises.name: Farmer Carry
exercises.note: Grip strength is total body strength — carry heavy, walk tall.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.note: Brace your core — stay rigid from head to toe.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
exercises.note: Breathe deep, decompress the spine.

exercises.name: Figure 4 Hip Stretch
exercises.note: Release the glutes and piriformis.

exercises.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
exercises.note: Open the chest — counteract all that pressing.

workouts.closingNote: Consistent work beats talent that doesn't show up. Stack these sessions and watch everything elevate.

workouts.title: No Equipment Bodyweight Grinder
workouts.coachNote: No gym? No excuse. Your body is the machine. Let's work.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.note: Drive those knees up — simulate sprint mechanics.

exercises.name: Leg Swings
exercises.note: Front-to-back and side-to-side.

exercises.name: Arm Crossover Stretch
exercises.note: Keep the shoulder mobile and healthy.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.note: Get the heart rate climbing gradually.

exercises.name: Jump Squats
exercises.note: Land soft — absorb force through your hips, not your knees.

exercises.name: Explosive Push-Ups
exercises.note: Push the floor away hard — train your fast-twitch fibers.

exercises.name: Broad Jump
exercises.note: Max distance each jump — reset your stance every rep.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.note: Drive the knees, stay flat in the hips.

exercises.name: Burpees
exercises.note: Chest to ground, full extension at the top.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunges
exercises.note: Step back, drop straight down — control the movement.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Ups
exercises.note: Builds overhead pressing strength without equipment.

exercises.name: Bear Crawl
exercises.note: Opposite hand and knee move together — stay low.

exercises.name: Standing Quad Stretch
exercises.note: Balance on one leg — stability training built right in.

exercises.name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
exercises.note: Flex your foot to deepen the stretch.

exercises.name: Thread-the-Needle Stretch
exercises.note: Opens the thoracic spine — important for all athletes.

workouts.closingNote: Work ethic is the one thing nobody can take from you. Great session today.

workouts.title: Dumbbell Strength Builder
workouts.coachNote: Master the basics with dumbbells first. Form now equals power later.

exercises.name: March in Place
exercises.note: Get the blood moving, stay loose.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
exercises.note: Hip flexor, thoracic rotation, and hamstring all in one.

exercises.name: Unloaded Good Morning
exercises.note: Teach the hinge pattern before you load it.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Goblet Squat
exercises.note: Hold the DB at your chest, elbows up, sit deep.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Keep the DBs close to your legs — feel the hamstrings load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Push Press
exercises.note: Slight dip and drive — teaches power transfer from legs to arms.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: Row to your hip, not your shoulder — full lat engagement.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Reverse Lunge
exercises.note: Control the step back, drive forward to stand.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Suitcase Carry
exercises.note: One DB, stay square — builds core anti-lateral stability.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.note: Lower back stays flat on the ground the entire time.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Hold
exercises.note: Arms overhead, lower back pressed down — feel the tension.

workouts.closingNote: Every elite athlete started with the basics. Respect the process and the results will come.

workouts.title: Upper Body Strength Day
workouts.coachNote: Upper body strength powers every sport — throwing, swinging, grappling, pushing off defenders. Build it right.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
exercises.note: Shoulder health essential — do this before every upper body session.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Retract the scapula fully on every rep.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension
exercises.note: Open up the mid-back for better pressing and pulling posture.

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Press
exercises.note: Upper chest power. Control the eccentric — 3 seconds down.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Neutral Grip Flat Press
exercises.note: Neutral grip reduces shoulder stress. Full stretch at the bottom.

exercises.name: Cable Chest Fly
exercises.note: Feel the squeeze across the chest. Slight bend in the elbow always.

exercises.name: Weighted Pull-Ups
exercises.note: Dead hang start, chin over bar, full extension at the bottom.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (Close Grip)
exercises.note: Row to the sternum, elbows tight to your sides.

exercises.name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.note: Brace your core hard — don't rotate the torso to cheat the rep.

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
exercises.note: Light weight, perfect form. Lead with the elbow.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
exercises.note: Full range of motion — don't cut the top short.

exercises.name: Tricep Rope Pushdown
exercises.note: Spread the rope at the bottom — full tricep extension.

exercises.name: External Rotation (Cable or Band)
exercises.note: Rotator cuff health. Light resistance, perfect form always.

workouts.closingNote: Strong upper body is a competitive advantage in every sport. You built it today.

workouts.title: Lower Body Explosion
workouts.coachNote: Every jump, sprint, cut, and change of direction starts from your legs. Build that foundation.

exercises.name: Leg Swing (Forward & Lateral)
exercises.note: Hold a wall for balance, keep the swing controlled.

exercises.name: Hip 90/90 Stretch
exercises.note: Internal and external hip rotation — critical for all athletes.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.note: Squeeze at the top for 2 seconds. Activate before you load.

exercises.name: Lateral Band Walk
exercises.note: Tension stays on the band the whole time.

exercises.name: Box Jump
exercises.note: Full effort every rep. Land soft and absorb. Step down — don't jump down.

exercises.name: Hang Power Clean
exercises.note: Triple extension — ankle, knee, hip. Explosive hip drive is the whole point.

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.note: Elbows high, upright torso. Front squat forces better posture.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: The most valuable single-leg exercise for athletes. Demanding but essential.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Full range, feet shoulder-width. Don't lock out the knees at the top.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.note: Top hamstring injury prevention drill. Slow eccentric — catch with your hands.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Glute power is sprint power. Drive straight up, hold 1 second at the top.

workouts.closingNote: Your legs are your engine. You just made them stronger. Keep stacking sessions.

workouts.title: Core & Stability Training
workouts.coachNote: Core training for athletes isn't about looks. It's about transferring power, protecting your spine, and staying strong under pressure.

exercises.name: Diaphragmatic Breathing
exercises.note: Belly rises on inhale. Learn to brace your core before any movement.

exercises.name: Dead Bug (Slow)
exercises.note: Opposite arm and leg lower simultaneously. Back stays flat the entire time.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.note: Breathe with each movement. Warm up the spine.

exercises.name: Plank with Shoulder Tap
exercises.note: Hips stay square. Resist the urge to rotate.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.note: Body in a straight line. Don't let the hips sag.

exercises.name: Pallof Press Hold (or Isometric)
exercises.note: Best anti-rotation drill for athletic core strength.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.note: Extend opposite arm and leg slowly. Hold 2 seconds at full extension.

exercises.name: V-Up
exercises.note: Simultaneous lift of arms and legs. Controlled, not swung.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunch
exercises.note: Slow it down — feel the oblique on every twist.

exercises.name: Hollow Body Rocks
exercises.note: Full body tension from head to toe. This is foundational core strength.

workouts.closingNote: A strong core makes every athletic movement more efficient. This is the work that shows up when it matters most.

workouts.title: Speed & Agility Field Session
workouts.coachNote: Speed is a skill. Agility is a skill. Both can be trained and improved with the right work.

exercises.name: A-Skip
exercises.note: Drive the knee, push the ground away with the opposite foot.

exercises.name: B-Skip
exercises.note: A-Skip plus leg extension. Pawing motion — not a kick.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.note: Arms drive hard. Rhythm and coordination are both being trained here.

exercises.name: Carioca
exercises.note: Hips rotate, feet stay quick. Great for lateral hip mobility.

exercises.name: Backpedal
exercises.note: Stay low, arms pump, weight forward on the balls of your feet.

exercises.name: 10-Yard Accelerations
exercises.note: First 3 steps win the play. Stay low, push the ground back.

exercises.name: 40-Yard Build-Up Sprint
exercises.note: Gradual acceleration — 50% to 75% to 100% over 40 yards.

exercises.name: Pro Agility (5-10-5)
exercises.note: Change of direction speed. Sink the hips on every cut.

exercises.name: L-Drill
exercises.note: Plant and cut. Your first step after the cut is everything.

exercises.name: Reactive Cone Drill
exercises.note: Coach points a direction — athlete reacts immediately. Trains reaction time.

exercises.name: Lateral Shuffle to Sprint
exercises.note: Shuffle 5 yards, plant, explode into a sprint. Simulates game cuts.

exercises.name: Cone Weave (Figure 8)
exercises.note: Body control and hip flexibility. Stay low through each gate.

workouts.closingNote: Reps build trust in your feet. Every drill today made you a faster, more confident athlete.

workouts.title: Conditioning Gauntlet
workouts.coachNote: Championships are won in the 4th quarter. Train your lungs and your legs like they matter — because they do.

exercises.name: Easy Jog
exercises.note: Easy pace — just get loose and warm.

exercises.name: Dynamic Stretching Circuit
exercises.note: Leg swings, hip circles, arm circles, trunk rotations.

exercises.name: 100-Yard Sprint
exercises.note: All out. Time yourself. Track your improvement week to week.

exercises.name: 10 Push-Ups (between sprints)
exercises.note: Strength endurance. Do these immediately after each sprint.

exercises.name: Pyramid Distance Run
exercises.note: Controlled effort — pace yourself up and back down the pyramid.

exercises.name: Walk Lap
exercises.note: Bring the heart rate down slow. Deep breathing throughout.

exercises.name: Calf Stretch
exercises.note: Hold a wall, heel pressed to the ground.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.note: Lunge position, reach the back arm overhead.

workouts.closingNote: Most athletes quit this workout mentally before their body gives out. You finished. That's the difference.

workouts.title: Youth Athlete Foundations
workouts.coachNote: We're building athletes from the ground up. Every drill today makes you faster, stronger, and more coordinated.

exercises.name: Jog in Place
exercises.note: Stay light on your feet, arms moving naturally.

exercises.name: Animal Walks (Bear, Crab, Duck)
exercises.note: Fun and effective — builds coordination and body awareness.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
exercises.note: Feet shoulder-width, chest up, sit back like you're sitting in a chair.

exercises.name: Push-Up (or Knee Push-Up)
exercises.note: Straight line from head to knee — no saggy hips.

exercises.name: Broad Jump
exercises.note: Swing the arms, jump as far as possible, stick the landing.

exercises.name: Lateral Shuffle
exercises.note: Stay low, don't cross your feet.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.note: Straight body, tight tummy — hold it like a board.

exercises.name: Sprint Relay
exercises.note: Compete. Make it fun. Fast starts win.

exercises.name: Jump & Clap
exercises.note: Jump as high as possible, clap above your head at the top.

workouts.closingNote: You worked hard today and that's something to be proud of. Same energy next session — let's keep building!

workouts.title: Elite Athlete Performance Protocol
workouts.coachNote: This session is built for athletes who want to play at the next level. Every movement has a purpose. Trust the process.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Your joints are your career. Protect them every single session.

exercises.name: Cable External Rotation
exercises.note: Elbow at 90 degrees, rotate out slowly and controlled.

exercises.name: Prone Y-T-W on Bench
exercises.note: Rear delt and rotator cuff strength. Light weight or bodyweight.

exercises.name: Foam Roller Thoracic Mobility
exercises.note: Open the mid-back — rotation and power transfer starts here.

exercises.name: Hang Power Clean
exercises.note: Triple extension is the key. This is functional athletic power.

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Rotational Slam
exercises.note: Full hip rotation, violent core contraction, controlled slam.

exercises.name: Depth Drop to Sprint
exercises.note: Step off box, absorb the landing, explode forward immediately.

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.note: More athletic than conventional deadlift. Full body power builder.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Balance plus hamstring strength — mimics single-leg athletic demands.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Incline Press
exercises.note: Upper chest and shoulder power without excessive joint stress.

exercises.name: Half-Kneeling Single-Arm Cable Row
exercises.note: Anti-rotation plus pulling strength — builds sport-specific stability.

exercises.name: Ladder Drills (Ickey Shuffle, In-Out)
exercises.note: Speed through the ladder — precision is the goal, not just pace.

exercises.name: Reactive Cone Change of Direction
exercises.note: Plant hard on the outside foot. First step out of the cut wins.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.note: Tight hip flexors kill speed and power. Stretch these after every session.

exercises.name: PNF Hamstring Stretch
exercises.note: Contract for 5 seconds, then relax into a deeper stretch.

exercises.name: Doorway Pec Stretch
exercises.note: Counteract all the pressing work from the session.

exercises.name: Neck and Trap Release
exercises.note: Slow and controlled side bends. Never force the neck.

workouts.closingNote: You trained like a professional today. Consistency over time is what separates good from great.

workouts.title: Kettlebell Athletic Circuit
workouts.coachNote: Kettlebells build functional athletic power. Every swing, press, and carry translates directly to sport performance.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Halo
exercises.note: Slow circles around the head. Loosens shoulders and thoracic spine.

exercises.name: KB Goblet Squat (light)
exercises.note: Perfect squat warm-up. Use this to groove the movement before loading.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
exercises.note: Hip hinge and drive — this is a hip thrust, not a squat. Stay explosive.

exercises.name: KB Clean & Press
exercises.note: Clean to rack, press overhead. Full hip extension drives the clean.

exercises.name: KB Goblet Squat
exercises.note: Deep and controlled. Stay upright. Feel the full squat movement.

exercises.name: KB Single-Leg Deadlift
exercises.note: Balance plus posterior chain strength. Essential for all athletes.

exercises.name: KB Overhead Waiter Carry
exercises.note: Shoulder stability under load. Stack the joint — ear, shoulder, hip, knee, ankle.

exercises.name: KB Windmill
exercises.note: Lateral hip strength and oblique stability combined in one movement.

exercises.name: KB Turkish Get-Up
exercises.note: Slow and deliberate. The ultimate full-body stability and coordination movement.

workouts.closingNote: Kettlebell work makes you feel athletic because it IS athletic. Great work today.

workouts.title: Resistance Band Total Body
workouts.coachNote: Bands are underrated tools. Constant tension throughout every movement means every inch of every rep counts.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Shoulder health first. Do this every session without exception.

exercises.name: Band Squat Walk
exercises.note: Band around ankles, keep tension throughout, stay low.

exercises.name: Banded Squat
exercises.note: Band under feet, hold at shoulders. Adds resistance at the top of the movement.

exercises.name: Banded Push-Up
exercises.note: Band across your back held in both hands — harder at the lockout.

exercises.name: Banded Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: Stand on band, hinge over, row to the hip.

exercises.name: Banded Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Band across the hips, anchored down. Full glute squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Banded Pallof Press
exercises.note: Anchor to a fixed point. Press straight out and resist rotation.

exercises.name: Banded Lateral Walk
exercises.note: Knees slightly bent, feet never fully together.

workouts.closingNote: Simple tools, serious results. The band never lies — if you felt it burn, you put in the work.

workouts.title: Home Gym Strength Session
workouts.coachNote: You don't need a fancy facility. You need consistency and effort. Let's get after it.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
exercises.note: Elevate the heart rate gradually.

exercises.name: Hip Circles
exercises.note: Loosen the hip joint in all planes of motion.

exercises.name: Push-Up to Downward Dog
exercises.note: Full upper body and posterior chain prep in one movement.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Hip Hinge
exercises.note: Groove the hinge pattern before loading it.

exercises.name: Dumbbell or Barbell Squat
exercises.note: Use whatever loading option you have. Depth and control matter most.

exercises.name: Push-Up Variation (weighted if possible)
exercises.note: Elevate feet for more difficulty or add a vest for load.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Deadlift
exercises.note: Hinge at the hips, keep the back flat, push the ground away.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: Control the movement in both directions — up and down.

exercises.name: Step-Up (using a chair or bench)
exercises.note: Drive through the heel of the elevated leg. Stay controlled.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.note: Squeeze everything. Glutes, abs, and quads all tight.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Lead with the elbow. Light weight and perfect form always wins.

exercises.name: Superman Hold
exercises.note: Builds lower back endurance and glute activation.

workouts.closingNote: Every session at home is a session your competition might be skipping. You showed up. That matters.

workouts.title: Push Day — Chest Shoulders Triceps
workouts.coachNote: Push sessions build the pressing power that translates to sport. Stay focused, feel every rep.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Activate the rear delts before all that pressing work.

exercises.name: Light Cable External Rotation
exercises.note: Warm up the rotator cuff — your shoulders will thank you.

exercises.name: Arm Circles
exercises.note: Big to small, forward and backward.

exercises.name: Barbell Flat Bench Press
exercises.note: Arch slightly, retract the scapula, drive the bar up and slightly back.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: 30-45 degree incline. Touch the dumbbells at the top on each rep.

exercises.name: Cable Fly (Low to High)
exercises.note: Arc the arms up like you're hugging a tree. Full chest stretch at the bottom.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.note: Full range — DBs at ear height at the bottom, touch lightly at the top.

exercises.name: Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Lead with the elbow, slight forward lean. No swinging.

exercises.name: Front Raise (alternating)
exercises.note: Control the descent — don't drop the weight back down.

exercises.name: Skull Crusher (EZ Bar)
exercises.note: Lower the bar to your forehead slowly. Elbows stay locked in place.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdown (Rope)
exercises.note: Spread the rope at the bottom. Full extension on every rep.

workouts.closingNote: Your push session is done. Recover well, eat right, and come back stronger.

workouts.title: Pull Day — Back & Biceps
workouts.coachNote: A strong back prevents injuries and builds the pulling power that matters in every sport.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (light)
exercises.note: Feel the lats engage. This is warm-up, not working sets.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
exercises.note: Always.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
exercises.note: Mobilize the spine before heavy pulling.

exercises.name: Pull-Ups (or Lat Pulldown)
exercises.note: Full range of motion. Dead hang at the bottom. Chin clears the bar.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
exercises.note: Hip hinge at 45 degrees. Row to the lower chest. Control the lower.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (Wide Grip)
exercises.note: Elbows flare out slightly — hits the upper back differently.

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Cable Pulldown
exercises.note: Isolates the lats without bicep involvement. Feel the pull in your back.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.note: Full supination at the top. Don't swing your body to lift.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Full stretch at the bottom — great for bicep peak development.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.note: Neutral grip builds the brachialis. Thickens the arm overall.

workouts.closingNote: Big back, strong pull, healthy shoulders. Every rep today made you a better athlete.

workouts.title: Mobility & Recovery Session
workouts.coachNote: Recovery is training. The athletes who last longest are the ones who treat their body right. This session is just as important as any other.

exercises.name: Box Breathing
exercises.note: Inhale 4s, hold 4s, exhale 4s, hold 4s. Calm the nervous system.

exercises.name: Supine Spinal Twist
exercises.note: Knee crosses the body, arms spread wide, breathe into the rotation.

exercises.name: 90/90 Hip Stretch
exercises.note: Sit tall, both legs at 90 degrees. Rotate through internal and external hip.

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose
exercises.note: Deepest hip flexor and glute release available. Breathe through the tension.

exercises.name: Standing Quad Stretch
exercises.note: Squeeze the glute of the standing leg for balance and activation.

exercises.name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
exercises.note: Flex the foot, reach for the toes without rounding the back.

exercises.name: Calf Stretch (wall)
exercises.note: Straight knee for upper calf, bent knee for soleus.

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Lunge Stretch
exercises.note: Sink the hips, reach the back arm overhead. Open the whole anterior chain.

exercises.name: Thread-the-Needle
exercises.note: Thoracic rotation opener. One arm sweeps under and through.

exercises.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
exercises.note: Elbow at 90 degrees, open the pec and anterior shoulder.

exercises.name: Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
exercises.note: Gentle rear delt and posterior shoulder release.

exercises.name: Neck Side Stretch
exercises.note: Ear drops toward shoulder. Never force. Always gentle.

workouts.closingNote: Your body just got the maintenance it needed. Mobility work today means performance tomorrow.
